text: In Chinese, '你好' means {{c1::Hello / Hi}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Nǐ hǎo}}.
explanation: '你好' (Nǐ hǎo) is the standard, universal greeting in Mandarin Chinese. It literally means 'You good?' and is used at any time of day.
usage:
Term: 你好 (Nǐ hǎo) | Use: General greeting for anyone
  • 你好! 我叫 Jesus。 — Hello! My name is Jesus.
spanish: En chino, '你好' significa 'Hola'. Pronunciación: Nǐ hǎo.

text: In Chinese, '早上好' means {{c1::Good morning}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Zǎo shang hǎo}}.
explanation: '早上好' (Zǎo shang hǎo) is a morning greeting used before noon. '早' (zǎo) alone is a casual shortcut, like saying 'Morning!'
usage:
Term: 早上好 (Zǎo shang hǎo) | Use: Morning greeting
  • 早上好! 今天天气真好。 — Good morning! The weather is beautiful today.
spanish: En chino, '早上好' significa 'Buenos días'. Pronunciación: Zǎo shang hǎo.

text: In Chinese, '晚上好' means {{c1::Good evening}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Wǎnshang hǎo}}.
explanation: '晚上好' (Wǎnshang hǎo) is used in the evening as a polite greeting. '晚' means 'late/evening'.
usage:
Term: 晚上好 (Wǎnshang hǎo) | Use: Evening greeting
  • 晚上好! 欢迎来到这里。 — Good evening! Welcome here.
spanish: En chino, '晚上好' significa 'Buenas noches'. Pronunciación: Wǎnshang hǎo.

text: In Chinese, '再见' means {{c1::Goodbye}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Zàijiàn}}.
explanation: '再见' (Zàijiàn) is the standard farewell. It literally means 'See again' (再=again, 见=see), similar to the French 'au revoir'.
usage:
Term: 再见 (Zàijiàn) | Use: Standard farewell
  • 再见! 明天见。 — Goodbye! See you tomorrow.
spanish: En chino, '再见' significa 'Adiós'. Pronunciación: Zàijiàn.

scenario: Chinese A1: Nice to Meet You 🗣️
text: In Chinese, '很高兴认识你' means {{c1::Nice to meet you}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Hěn gāoxìng rènshí nǐ}}.
explanation: '很高兴认识你' is the standard phrase when meeting someone for the first time. It literally means 'Very happy to know you'.
usage:
Term: 很高兴认识你 | Use: First meeting
  • 很高兴认识你! 你是哪里人? — Nice to meet you! Where are you from?
spanish: En chino, '很高兴认识你' significa 'Mucho gusto'. Pronunciación: Hěn gāoxìng rènshí nǐ.

text: In Chinese, '你好吗?' means {{c1::How are you?}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Nǐ hǎo ma?}}.
explanation: '你好吗?' adds the question particle '吗' (ma) to the greeting '你好'. A common response is '我很好' (Wǒ hěn hǎo) — I'm very well.
usage:
Term: 你好吗? (Nǐ hǎo ma?)
  • A: 你好吗? — B: 我很好，谢谢! (I'm very well, thank you!)
spanish: En chino, '你好吗?' significa '¿Cómo estás?'. Pronunciación: Nǐ hǎo ma?

text: In Chinese, '谢谢' means {{c1::Thank you}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Xièxie}}.
explanation: '谢谢' (Xièxie) is the most common way to express gratitude. A polite response is '不客气' (Bú kèqi) — You're welcome.
usage:
Term: 谢谢 (Xièxie)
  • A: 谢谢! — B: 不客气! (You're welcome!)
spanish: En chino, '谢谢' significa 'Gracias'. Pronunciación: Xièxie.

text: In Chinese, '不客气' means {{c1::You're welcome}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Bú kèqi}}.
explanation: '不客气' (Bú kèqi) literally means 'don't be polite'. It's the standard response to '谢谢'. Another common alternative is '没事' (Méi shì) — No problem.
usage:
Term: 不客气 (Bú kèqi)
  • A: 谢谢你的帮助! — B: 不客气!
spanish: En chino, '不客气' significa 'De nada'. Pronunciación: Bú kèqi.

text: In Chinese, '对不起' means {{c1::Sorry / I apologize}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Duìbuqǐ}}.
explanation: '对不起' (Duìbuqǐ) is used for sincere apologies. For a lighter 'excuse me' to get someone's attention, use '不好意思' (Bù hǎoyìsi).
usage:
Term: 对不起 (Duìbuqǐ)
  • 对不起，我来晚了。 — Sorry, I'm late.
spanish: En chino, '对不起' significa 'Lo siento'. Pronunciación: Duìbuqǐ.

text: In Chinese, '明天见' means {{c1::See you tomorrow}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Míngtiān jiàn}}.
explanation: '明天见' combines '明天' (míngtiān = tomorrow) and '见' (jiàn = see). This structure is flexible: '下周见' = see you next week.
usage:
Term: 明天见 (Míngtiān jiàn)
  • 好的，明天见! — OK, see you tomorrow!
spanish: En chino, '明天见' significa 'Hasta mañana'. Pronunciación: Míngtiān jiàn.

text: In Chinese, '晚安' means {{c1::Good night}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Wǎn'ān}}.
explanation: '晚安' (Wǎn'ān) is used as a farewell at night or before bed. '晚' = evening/late, '安' = peace/safe.
usage:
Term: 晚安 (Wǎn'ān)
  • 晚安，好梦! — Good night, sweet dreams!
spanish: En chino, '晚安' significa 'Buenas noches (al despedirse)'. Pronunciación: Wǎn'ān.

text: In Chinese, '欢迎' means {{c1::Welcome}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Huānyíng}}.
explanation: '欢迎' (Huānyíng) is used to welcome guests or visitors. Shops and restaurants often display '欢迎光临' (Huānyíng guānglín) — Welcome (honored guest).
usage:
Term: 欢迎 (Huānyíng)
  • 欢迎来到北京! — Welcome to Beijing!
spanish: En chino, '欢迎' significa 'Bienvenido'. Pronunciación: Huānyíng.

scenario: Chinese A1: Long Time No See 🗣️
text: In Chinese, '好久不见' means {{c1::Long time no see}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Hǎo jiǔ bù jiàn}}.
explanation: Interestingly, the English phrase 'long time no see' is believed to be a direct calque from the Chinese '好久不见'. It is widely used in casual reunions.
usage:
Term: 好久不见 (Hǎo jiǔ bù jiàn)
  • 好久不见！你最近怎么样? — Long time no see! How have you been lately?
spanish: En chino, '好久不见' significa '¡Cuánto tiempo sin verte!'. Pronunciación: Hǎo jiǔ bù jiàn.

text: In Chinese, '请' means {{c1::Please}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Qǐng}}.
explanation: '请' (Qǐng) can mean 'please', 'invite', or 'treat (to a meal)'. As a polite particle it's placed at the start of requests: '请坐' = Please sit down.
usage:
Term: 请 (Qǐng)
  • 请跟我来。 — Please follow me.
  • 请问，洗手间在哪里? — Excuse me, where is the restroom?
spanish: En chino, '请' significa 'Por favor'. Pronunciación: Qǐng.

scenario: Chinese A1: What is Your Name 🗣️
text: In Chinese, '你叫什么名字?' means {{c1::What is your name?}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Nǐ jiào shénme míngzi?}}
explanation: '你叫什么名字?' — literally 'You are called what name?' — is the standard way to ask someone's name. The answer is '我叫…' (Wǒ jiào…) — My name is…
usage:
Term: 你叫什么名字?
  • A: 你叫什么名字? — B: 我叫 Jesus。(My name is Jesus.)
spanish: En chino, '你叫什么名字?' significa '¿Cómo te llamas?'. Pronunciación: Nǐ jiào shénme míngzi?

scenario: Chinese A1: Where Are You From 🗣️
text: In Chinese, '你是哪里人?' means {{c1::Where are you from?}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Nǐ shì nǎlǐ rén?}}
explanation: '你是哪里人?' literally means 'You are where person?' The answer is '我是墨西哥人' (Wǒ shì Mòxīgē rén) — I am Mexican.
usage:
Term: 你是哪里人?
  • A: 你是哪里人? — B: 我是墨西哥人。(I'm Mexican.)
spanish: En chino, '你是哪里人?' significa '¿De dónde eres?'. Pronunciación: Nǐ shì nǎlǐ rén?

text: In Chinese, '我听不懂' means {{c1::I don't understand (listening)}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Wǒ tīng bu dǒng}}.
explanation: '我听不懂' specifically means 'I cannot understand what I heard'. For not understanding written text, use '我看不懂'. A useful learner phrase.
usage:
Term: 我听不懂 (Wǒ tīng bu dǒng)
  • 对不起，我听不懂。请说慢一点。 — Sorry, I don't understand. Please speak more slowly.
spanish: En chino, '我听不懂' significa 'No entiendo (lo que escucho)'. Pronunciación: Wǒ tīng bu dǒng.

scenario: Chinese A1: Can You Speak Slower 🗣️
text: In Chinese, '请说慢一点' means {{c1::Please speak more slowly}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Qǐng shuō màn yīdiǎn}}.
explanation: An essential phrase for language learners. '慢' (màn) = slow, '一点' (yīdiǎn) = a little. Together: 'a little slower'.
usage:
Term: 请说慢一点
  • 你能说慢一点吗? — Can you speak a little slower?
spanish: En chino, '请说慢一点' significa 'Por favor habla más despacio'. Pronunciación: Qǐng shuō màn yīdiǎn.

scenario: Chinese A1: Do You Speak English 🗣️
text: In Chinese, '你会说英语吗?' means {{c1::Do you speak English?}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Nǐ huì shuō Yīngyǔ ma?}}
explanation: '会说' (huì shuō) means 'can speak'. '英语' is English. To ask about Spanish: '你会说西班牙语吗?' (Nǐ huì shuō Xībānyáyǔ ma?)
usage:
Term: 你会说英语吗?
  • A: 你会说英语吗? — B: 会一点。(A little.)
spanish: En chino, '你会说英语吗?' significa '¿Hablas inglés?'. Pronunciación: Nǐ huì shuō Yīngyǔ ma?

text: In Chinese, '我很好' means {{c1::I'm very well / I'm fine}}. Pronunciation: {{c2::Wǒ hěn hǎo}}.
explanation: '我很好' is the standard positive response to '你好吗?'. '很' (hěn) intensifies the adjective — it is almost always required in such sentences.
usage:
Term: 我很好 (Wǒ hěn hǎo)
  • A: 你好吗? — B: 我很好，谢谢! 你呢? (I'm well, thank you! And you?)
spanish: En chino, '我很好' significa 'Estoy muy bien'. Pronunciación: Wǒ hěn hǎo.
